channel.title: china-static:middleeast
channel.description: Static RSS merge for china variant (middleeast)

item.title: Kenyan Health Minister orders halt of construction of US Ebola facility
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Kenya’s Health Minister has stopped the construction of a US Ebola facility in Kenya.

item.title: Ghana celebrates after holding nervous England to 0-0 draw
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Ghanaians took to the streets in celebration after the Black Stars held England to a 0-0 draw at the World Cup.

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes first player to score in six World Cups
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice in Portugal’s 5-0 win over Uzbekistan

item.title: Kilts and bagpipes flood Miami streets as Scotland prepare to face Brazil
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Kilts and bagpipes flood Miami streets as Scotland prepare to face Brazil.

item.title: Munoz sends Colombia into World Cup knockouts with 1-0 win over DR Congo
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Daniel Munoz's 76th-minute goal guided Colombia to a Group K win over DR Congo, sending them to the round of 32.

item.title: NBA Draft 2026: Wizards select teen sensation AJ Dybantsa with No. 1 pick
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The 6ft 8in (2.03m) 19-year-old forward was scooped by the Wizards after playing just one season of college basketball.

item.title: Which teams have qualified for the World Cup 2026 knockouts, round of 32?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup knockout stage format, criteria and rules for qualification. Find out who's in and who's out.

item.title: China takes US crown on ranking of world’s fastest supercomputers
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: China’s LineShine overtakes US-based El Capitan as most powerful supercomputer, according to the TOP500 list.

item.title: Trump claims US recouped Venezuela war costs 28 times over
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US President Donald Trump claimed the United States has recovered its costs 28 times over through oil extraction.

item.title: Prada plays a part in designing NASA’s lunar mission spacesuits
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: NASA astronauts plan to return to the moon in 2028 wearing spacesuits made with more safety features and mobility.

item.title: Israel denying Palestinian women medical care in prison
item.dc:creator: Middle East Eye
item.description: Israel denying Palestinian women medical care in prison Palestinian women are facing “catastrophic” conditions in Israel’s Damon Prison, according to a report by the Palestinian Prisoners and Ex-Prisoners Affairs Commission. The Commission cited severe overcrowding, deliberate medical neglect, and the denial of basic human rights. Damon Prison, which currently holds 87 Palestinian women, is also reportedly denying pregnant prisoners necessary media care and basic necessities. The Commission added that women are only allowed outside their cells for one hour per day for recreation and showering,

item.title: Ben-Gvir says Israel may ‘act alone’ against Iran in split with US
item.dc:creator: Middle East Eye
item.description: Ben-Gvir says Israel may ‘act alone’ against Iran in split with US Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir said on Tuesday that the US would be “very naive” if it expects Iran to abandon its nuclear programme, suggesting that Israel may need to “act alone” in confronting Tehran. “The Americans are very naive if they think the Iranians will abandon their nuclear program and cancel it, and give up their dreams of destroying Israel,” he said during an interview with Israel’s Channel 7 news, adding that “it is Israel’s responsibility to confront this Iranian threat and act against it al

item.title: UN report: Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children
item.dc:creator: Middle East Eye
item.description: UN report: Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children A UN commission of inquiry released on Tuesday found that Israel had committed genocide in Gaza due to the deliberate targeting of Palestinian children. “The evidence shows that Palestinian children have been deliberately targeted and killed by the Israeli security forces,” said Srinivasan Muralidhar, Chair of the Commission. “Even after the October 2025 ceasefire, children continue to be killed and seriously injured, with continued disregard by Israel for the ceasefire and for the protection owed to Palestinian c

item.title: Oman announces temporary maritime corridor to bypass Hormuz blockade
item.dc:creator: Middle East Eye
item.description: Oman announces temporary maritime corridor to bypass Hormuz blockade Oman said on Tuesday that it had coordinated with the International Maritime Organisation (IMO) to provide a temporary maritime corridor for vessels seeking to transit through the Strait of Hormuz, Oman's state news agency reported. It added that vessels wishing to pass through would need to coordinate with the IMO and Omani authorities.

item.title: UN report: 80 percent of Gaza living in dire conditions
item.dc:creator: Middle East Eye
item.description: UN report: 80 percent of Gaza living in dire conditions Nearly 1.7 million displaced Palestinians - about 80 percent of Gaza’s population - are living with acute shortages of water, shelter and basic services, according to an assessment of displacement sites from the UN’s Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs. A spokesperson for the UN chief added that at least 59,000 shelters house more than eight people each, and around 38,500 people are sleeping in the open, while an estimated 600,000 people lack sufficient access to drinking water. Children watch as smoke billows in the backg

item.title: Iran's ballistic missiles were 'never on the table' in US talks
item.dc:creator: Middle East Eye
item.description: Iran's ballistic missiles were 'never on the table' in US talks Submitted by MEE staff on Tue, 06/23/2026 - 16:53 Pakistan says one of the main objectives of the US-Israel war on Iran was not even a topic of discussion during negotiations Iran’s President Masoud Pezeshkian shaking hands with Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif during his ceremonial reception at the Prime Minister House in Islamabad on 23 June 2026 (Pakistan's Prime Minister Office/AFP) Off Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said on Tuesday that Iran's ballistic missiles were "never on the table" during US -Iran talk

item.title: Israeli forces demolish Palestinian home in occupied West Bank
item.dc:creator: Middle East Eye
item.description: Israeli forces demolish Palestinian home in occupied West Bank Israeli forces demolished a Palestinian home in the eastern area of Hebron in the occupied West Bank on Tuesday, Wafa news reported. Local sources said that Israeli soldiers, accompanied by heavy machinery, knocked down a two-story building which was home to two families, including children and elderly residents, who have been left homeless following the demolition. According to Palestinian security sources, the orders were issued on the grounds that the homes lacked Israeli-issued building permits. Israeli bulldozers guarded by Is

item.title: US Senate votes to halt Iran war
item.dc:creator: Middle East Eye
item.description: US Senate votes to halt Iran war The Republican-majority US Senate backed legislation on Tuesday to stop military action against Iran, reflecting the growing unpopularity of the war as peace negotiations continue. The Senate voted 50-48 in favour of the resolution, which passed the House of Representatives in early June. Polling suggests that only one in four Americans believe that the Iran war was worth the cost to the US, affecting Trump’s popularity, which has fallen to record low levels to match the worst recorded in his second term.

item.title: US temporarily eases restrictions on Iran’s World Cup team
item.dc:creator: Middle East Eye
item.description: US temporarily eases restrictions on Iran’s World Cup team The United States has eased restrictions on Iran’s football team for the World Cup, allowing them to travel into the US two days before their next match rather than one, according to the US Department of Homeland Security (DHS). The restrictions placed on the players had led the Iranian coach to say that his country’s was “the most oppressed team in the whole World Cup”. A DHS spokesperson said that the Iranian team will still be required to leave the day the match ends. Iranian football players warm up before the FIFA World Cup 2026 m

item.title: Without missiles Iran would have ended up ‘like Gaza’, Iranian president says
item.dc:creator: Middle East Eye
item.description: Without missiles Iran would have ended up ‘like Gaza’, Iranian president says Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said on Tuesday that if Iran did not have its missile defence programme, it would have ended up “razed like Gaza” in the US-Israeli war. “If the missiles we have for our defence did not exist, Israel and the United States would have razed Iran like Gaza,” Pezeshkian said, reiterating that Iran’s defensive capabilities would not be negotiated “under any circumstances”.

item.title: Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say
item.dc:creator: BBC Middle East
item.description: Israel's military says the two men were Hezbollah operatives, but the Iran-backed group accuses it of a ceasefire violation.

item.title: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
item.dc:creator: BBC Middle East
item.description: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

item.title: UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children
item.dc:creator: BBC Middle East
item.description: Israel rejects the new report by the three-member expert panel, calling it a "libellous sham".

item.title: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back
item.dc:creator: BBC Middle East
item.description: Iran's foreign ministry says it made "no new commitments" on nuclear inspections after talks in Switzerland.

item.title: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
item.dc:creator: BBC Middle East
item.description: The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.

item.title: First round of US-Iran talks ends with encouraging progress, mediators say
item.dc:creator: BBC Middle East
item.description: The US-Iran talks began on Sunday in Switzerland, after last week's agreement, which includes a commitment to reach a final deal within 60 days.

item.title: Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
item.dc:creator: BBC Middle East
item.description: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

item.title: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike
item.dc:creator: BBC Middle East
item.description: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died after several days in hospital, an environmental group said.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah continue strikes despite ceasefire agreement
item.dc:creator: BBC Middle East
item.description: At least 20 people have reportedly been killed by Israeli air strikes as the Israeli military said Hezbollah had fired at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Lebanon says Israeli strikes kill 47 as Israel says four soldiers killed by Hezbollah
item.dc:creator: BBC Middle East
item.description: It comes a day after the US and Iran signed a deal to end their conflict, including fighting in Lebanon.
